content: The solution of the differential equation $\frac{d y}{d x}+x \sin 2 y=x^3 \cos ^2 y$ is
explanation:
We have $\frac{d y}{d x}+x \sin 2 y=x^3 \cos ^2 y$

Dividing both sides by $\cos ^2 y$, we get

$$ \begin{aligned} & \sec ^2 y \frac{d y}{d x}+x \frac{\sin 2 y}{\cos ^2 y}=x^3 \\ & \Rightarrow \sec ^2 y \frac{d y}{d x}+2 x \tan y=x^3 \end{aligned} $$

Put $\tan y=v$

and $\sec ^2 y \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d v}{d x}$, we get

$$ \frac{d v}{d x}+(2 x) v=x^3 $$

Now, $\mathrm{IF}=e^{\int 2 x d x}=e^{x^2}$

Multiplying both sides by IF, we get

$$ e^{x^2} \frac{d v}{d x}+2 x v \cdot e^{x^2}=e^{x^2} \cdot x^3 $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { Integrating both sides w.r.t } x \text {, we get }\\ &\begin{aligned} & v e^{x^2}=\int x^3 e^{x^2} d x+C \\ &=\int x^3 e^t\left(\frac{d t}{2 x}\right)+C, \text { where } t=x^2 \\ &=\frac{1}{2} \int t e^t d t+C=\frac{1}{2}\left(t e^t-e^t\right)+C \\ &=\frac{1}{2}(t-1) e^t+C=\frac{1}{2}\left(x^2-1\right) e^{x^2}+C \\ & \Rightarrow \quad e^{x^2} \tan y=\frac{1}{2}\left(x^2-1\right) e^{x^2}+C \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

content:
The solution of the differential equation

$ (x+1) \frac{d y}{d x}-y=e^{3 x}(x+1)^{2} $ is
explanation:
To solve the given differential equation

$ (x+1) \frac{d y}{d x} - y = e^{3x}(x+1)^2 $

we start by rewriting it in the standard linear form:

$ \frac{d y}{d x} - \frac{y}{x+1} = e^{3x}(x+1) $

Here, it's a first-order linear differential equation of the form

$ \frac{d y}{d x} + P(x)y = Q(x) $

where $ P(x) = -\frac{1}{x+1} $ and $ Q(x) = e^{3x}(x+1) $.

Step 1: Calculate the Integrating Factor (IF)

The integrating factor is given by:

$ \text{IF} = e^{\int P(x) \, dx} = e^{\int -\frac{1}{x+1} \, dx} = e^{-\ln(x+1)} = \frac{1}{x+1} $

Step 2: Determine the Solution

Using the integrating factor, the solution of the differential equation is:

$ y \cdot \text{IF} = \int Q(x) \cdot \text{IF} \, dx $

Substituting the known values:

$ y \cdot \frac{1}{x+1} = \int e^{3x}(x+1) \cdot \frac{1}{x+1} \, dx $

This simplifies to:

$ \frac{y}{x+1} = \frac{e^{3x}}{3} + C' $

Therefore, the complete solution is:

$ \frac{3y}{x+1} = e^{3x} + C' $

where $ C' = C/3 $ representing an integration constant.

content: If $$\left(1+x^2\right) d y+2 x y d x=\cot x d x$$, then the general solution be
explanation:
Given, $$(1+x)^2 d y+2 x y d x=\cot x d x$$

$$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}+\frac{2 x}{1+x^2} y=\frac{\cot x}{1+x^2}$$, which is a linear differential equation of the form of $$\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q$$

Here, $$P=\frac{2 x}{1+x^2}$$ and $$Q=\frac{\cot x}{1+x^2}$$

Now, IF $$=\exp \left(\int \frac{2 x}{1+x^2} d x\right)=\exp \left(\log \left(1+x^2\right)\right)$$

$$=1+x^2$$

and the solution of this linear differential equation be

$$\begin{aligned} & y \times \mathrm{IF}=\int(Q \times \mathrm{IF}) d x+C \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y\left(1+x^2\right)=\int\left(\frac{\cot x}{1+x^2} \times\left(1+x^2\right)\right) d x+C \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y\left(1+x^2\right)=\int \cot x d x+C \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y\left(1+x^2\right)=\log |\sin x|+C \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y=\frac{\log |\sin x|}{1+x^2}+\frac{C}{1+x^2} \end{aligned}$$

content: $$\left( {{{dy} \over {dx}}} \right)\tan x = y{\sec ^2}x + \sin x$$, find general solution

options.content: $$y = \tan x(\log |{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} x - \cot x| + \cos x + c)$$

explanation:
Given that

$$\left( {{{dy} \over {dx}}} \right)\tan x = y{\sec ^2}x + \sin x$$

$$\tan \left( {{{dy} \over {dx}}} \right) - y{\sec ^2}x = \sin x$$

$${{dy} \over {dx}} + \left( {{{ - {{\sec }^2}x} \over {\tan x}}} \right)y = {{\sin x} \over {\tan x}}$$

Here, $$P = - {{{{\sec }^2}x} \over {\tan x}}$$ and $$Q = \cos x$$

$$IF = {e^{\int {P\,dx} }} = {e^{\int {{{ - {{\sec }^2}x} \over {\tan x}}dx} }}$$

$$IF = {e^{ - \log (\tan x)}} = {e^{\log \cot x}} = \cot x$$

Now, $$y \times IF = \int {(Q \times IF)dx + C} $$

$$y\,.\,\cot x = \int {\cos x\,.\,\cot x + C} $$

$$y\,.\,\cot x = \int {{{1 - {{\sin }^2}x} \over {\sin x}} + C} $$

$$y\,.\,\cot x = \log |{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} \,x - \cot x| + \cos x + C$$

content: Solution of $$\left( {{{x + y - 1} \over {x + y - 2}}} \right){{dy} \over {dx}} = \left( {{{x + y + 1} \over {x + y + 2}}} \right)$$, given that y = 1 when x = 1 is
explanation:
$$\left( {{{x + y - 1} \over {x + y - 2}}} \right){{dy} \over {dx}} = \left( {{{x + y + 1} \over {x + y + 2}}} \right)$$

Put $$x + y = v$$

$$\therefore$$ $$1 + {{dy} \over {dx}} = {{dv} \over {dx}}$$ or $$\left( {{{v - 1} \over {v - 2}}} \right)\left( {{{dv} \over {dx}} - 1} \right) = \left( {{{v + 1} \over {v + 2}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{dv} \over {dx}} - 1 = {{(v + 1)(v - 2)} \over {(v - 1)(v + 2)}} = {{{v^2} - v - 2} \over {{v^2} + v - 2}}$$ or $${{dv} \over {dx}} = {{2{v^2} - 4} \over {({v^2} + v - 2)}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{({v^2} + v - 2)} \over {({v^2} - 2)}}dv = 2dx \Rightarrow \left( {1 + {v \over {{v^2} - 2}}} \right)dv = 2dx$$

On integrating, we get

$$v + {1 \over 2}\log \left| {{v^2} - 2} \right| = 2x + c$$

or, $$(y - x) + {1 \over 2}\log \left| {{{(x + y)}^2} - 2} \right| = c$$

Given, y = 1 when x = 1

$$\therefore$$ $$0 + {1 \over 2}\log 2 = c$$ or $$(y - x) + {1 \over 2}\log \left| {{{{{(x + y)}^2} - 2} \over 2}} \right| = 0$$

or, $$\log \left| {{{{{(x + y)}^2} - 2} \over 2}} \right| = 2(x - y)$$

content: The solution of $${x^3}{{dy} \over {dx}} + 4{x^2}\tan y = {e^x}\sec y$$ satisfying y (1) = 0, is
explanation:
We have, $$\cos y{{dy} \over {dx}} + {4 \over x}\sin y = {{{e^x}} \over {{x^3}}}$$

Let $$\sin y = t$$

$$ \Rightarrow \cos y{{dy} \over {dx}} = {{dt} \over {dx}}$$ $$\therefore$$ $${{dt} \over {dx}} + {4 \over x}t = {{{e^x}} \over {{x^3}}}$$

$$IF = {e^{\int {{4 \over x}dx} }} = {e^{4\log x}} = {x^4}$$

The solution is

$$\therefore$$ $$t{x^4} = \int {{x^4}\,.\,{{{e^x}} \over {{x^3}}}dx = x{e^x} - {e^x} + c} $$ $$\because$$ $$\sin y\,{x^4} = x{e^x} - {e^x} + c$$

$$\because$$ x = 1, y = 0 [$$\because$$ c = 0]

$$\therefore$$ $$\sin y = {e^x}(x - 1){x^{ - 4}}$$

content: The solution of the equation $${{dy} \over {dx}} + {1 \over x}\tan y = {1 \over {{x^2}}}\tan y\sin y$$ is
explanation:
We have, $${{dy} \over {dx}} + {{\tan y} \over x} = {{\tan y\sin y} \over {{x^2}}}$$

Now, divide by tan y sin y both sides, we get

$$\cot y\cos ec\,y{{dy} \over {dx}} + {{\cos ec\,y} \over x} = {1 \over {{x^2}}}$$ ...... (i)

Put cosec y = z

$$\therefore$$ $$ - \cos ec\,y\cot y{{dy} \over {dx}} = {{dz} \over {dx}}$$

$$\therefore$$ Eq. (i) reduces to

$${{dz} \over {dx}} - {z \over x} = - {1 \over {{x^2}}}$$

$$IF = {e^{\int { - {1 \over x}dx} }} = {e^{ - \log x}} = {1 \over x}$$

Hence solution is

$${z \over x} = \int { - {1 \over {{x^3}}}dx = {1 \over {2{x^2}}} + c} $$

$$ \Rightarrow {1 \over {x\sin y}} = {{1 - 2c{x^2}} \over {2{x^2}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 2x = \sin y{(1 - 2cx)^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 2x = \sin y(1 - 2c{x^2})$$

content: The solution of differential equation $$(x{y^5} + 2y)dx - xdy = 0$$, is
explanation:
We have, $$(x{y^5} + 2y)dx = x\,dy$$

$$ \Rightarrow x{{dy} \over {dx}} - 2y = x{y^5}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{dy} \over {dx}} - {{2y} \over x} = {y^5}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {y^{ - 5}}{{dy} \over {dx}} - {{2{y^{ - 4}}} \over x} = 1$$ ..... (i)

Put, $${y^{ - 4}} = t$$

$$ \Rightarrow - 4{y^{ - 5}}{{dy} \over {dx}} = {{dt} \over {dx}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {y^{ - 5}}{{dy} \over {dx}} = {{ - 1} \over 4}{{dt} \over {dx}}$$ ..... (ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$$ - {1 \over 4}{{dt} \over {dx}} - {{2t} \over x} = 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{dt} \over {dx}} + {{8t} \over x} = - 4$$

Now, $$IF = {e^{\int {{8 \over x}dx} }} = {e^{8\log x}} = {x^8}$$

and the solution is

$$t\,.\,{x^8} = \int {( - 4){x^8}dx + C} $$

$$ \Rightarrow {{{x^8}} \over {{y^4}}} = - {{4\,.\,{x^9}} \over 9} + C$$

$$ \Rightarrow 9{x^8} + 4{x^9}\,.\,{y^4} = 9{y^4}C$$

content: A curve passes through (2, 0) and the slope of the tangent at P(x, y) is equal to $${{{{(x + 1)}^2} + y - 3} \over {x + 1}}$$ then the equation of the curve is
explanation:
We have,

Slope of tangent of the curve is $${{{{(x + 1)}^2} + y - 3} \over {x + 1}}$$

$$\therefore$$ $${{dy} \over {dx}} = {{{{(x + 1)}^2} + y - 3} \over {x + 1}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{dy} \over {dx}} = {{{{(x + 1)}^2} - 3} \over {x + 1}} + {y \over {x + 1}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{dy} \over {dx}} - {y \over {x + 1}} = {{{{(x + 1)}^2} - 3} \over {x + 1}}$$

This is a form of linear differential equations

$$\therefore$$ $$IF = {e^{\int {{{ - 1} \over {x + 1}}dx} }} = {e^{\log {1 \over {1 + x}}}} = {1 \over {1 + x}}$$

Solution of given differential equation is

$${y \over {x + 1}} = \int {{{{{(x + 1)}^2} - 3} \over {{{(x + 1)}^2}}}dx} $$

$${y \over {x + 1}} = \int {\left( {1 - {3 \over {{{(x + 1)}^2}}}} \right)dx} $$

$${y \over {x + 1}} = x + {3 \over {x + 1}} + c$$

$$\because$$ Curve passes through (2, 0)

$$\therefore$$ $$0 = 2 + {3 \over 3} + c$$

$$ \Rightarrow c = - 3$$

Hence, equation of curve is

$${y \over {x + 1}} = x + {3 \over {x + 1}} - 3$$

$$y = {x^2} + x + 3 - 3x - 3$$

$$y = {x^2} - 2x$$
